text: Hey there! I'm Newsie, your AI news companion. I just saw that the Reserve Bank of India has cut the repo rate by 25 basis points to 6 percent. This is the second consecutive rate cut, aimed at boosting economic growth amid global uncertainties. Analysts expect this could lower EMI burdens for home loan borrowers.

Great question! Speaking of the markets, the Sensex surged over 600 points today, crossing the 77,000 mark for the first time. The rally was led by banking and IT stocks. Nifty also gained 1.2 percent, closing above 23,400. Foreign institutional investors turned net buyers after weeks of selling.

Absolutely! In other major news, India's GDP growth for Q3 came in at 6.2 percent, beating market expectations of 5.8 percent. The manufacturing sector showed strong recovery, while the services sector continued its robust performance. The government remains confident of achieving 7 percent growth for the full fiscal year.

That's an interesting point! On the global front, the US Federal Reserve has signaled a pause in rate hikes, keeping the benchmark rate steady at 5.25 to 5.5 percent. Fed Chair Jerome Powell indicated that future decisions will be data-dependent, with inflation still running above the 2 percent target.

You raise a good point! In the tech world, India's IT sector is seeing a wave of AI-driven transformation. Major companies like TCS, Infosys, and Wipro have reported increased deal wins in AI and cloud services. The sector is expected to add over 150,000 new jobs in the AI domain this fiscal year.

Interesting perspective! Let me share another update — crude oil prices have dropped to 72 dollars per barrel following OPEC's decision to gradually increase production. This is positive news for India, as lower oil prices help control inflation and reduce the current account deficit.

That's a great observation! On the startup front, India has crossed the 110 unicorn milestone. The latest entrants include companies in fintech, healthtech, and clean energy. The startup ecosystem raised over 12 billion dollars in funding this quarter, showing strong investor confidence.

You're absolutely right! In the real estate sector, housing sales in the top 7 cities have surged by 30 percent year-on-year. Mumbai and Bengaluru led the growth, driven by lower interest rates and festive season demand. Affordable housing remains the biggest demand driver across markets.
